decision_summary: The chip temperature (89.26°C) exceeds the 85.0°C limit. Previous attempts to increase spreader conductivity hit the upper bound (500.0). The strategy shifts to geometric expansion of the heat spreader to increase the cross-sectional area for lateral heat spreading. We will increase the spreader width by ~11% (within the 1.5x limit) to better cover the domain and pull heat away from the chip.

changes.reason: Increasing the heat spreader width maximizes the lateral area available to conduct heat towards the cooled boundaries, reducing thermal resistance without exceeding material conductivity bounds.

expected_effects: Reduction in chip peak temperature due to lower lateral thermal resistance.
More uniform temperature distribution across the top layer.
Movement of the design towards feasibility (Temp <= 85.0°C).
risk_notes: The spreader width is approaching the design domain limit (1.2m); future width increases may be constrained.
If the temperature reduction is insufficient, subsequent steps may require increasing base plate conductivity or adjusting vertical positioning if clearance allows.
